# anyscale_service (Data Source)

Fetches details about an Anyscale Service by ID or name. Use this data source to look up a service - one deployed by the `anyscale_service` resource, or one deployed and managed some other way (e.g. the Anyscale CLI or console) - without taking over its lifecycle. To create a service or roll out new versions from Terraform, use the `anyscale_service` resource instead; this data source stays read-only.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Look up by ID
data "anyscale_service" "by_id" {
  id = "service2_abc123"
}

# Look up by name. Service names are unique only WITHIN A PROJECT, not
# organization-wide, so if two different projects each have a service called
# "my-service" this lookup errors out asking you to set project_id (and/or
# cloud_id) to disambiguate - it never silently guesses which one you meant
# the way this provider's other by-name lookups (cloud, project, compute
# config, container image) pick the most recently created match.
data "anyscale_service" "by_name" {
  name       = "my-service"
  project_id = "prj_abc123" # only required if "my-service" isn't unique org-wide
}

output "service_state" {
  value       = data.anyscale_service.by_id.current_state
  description = "Current lifecycle state, e.g. RUNNING, UNHEALTHY, TERMINATED"
}

output "service_base_url" {
  value       = data.anyscale_service.by_id.base_url
  description = "The base URL clients use to reach this service"
}

# canary_version is null except while a rollout is actively in progress -
# check for null before reading fields off it.
output "service_is_rolling_out" {
  value       = data.anyscale_service.by_id.canary_version != null
  description = "Whether a canary rollout is currently in progress"
}

# ray_serve_config is a dynamic, open-ended JSON blob upstream (no fixed
# schema - it can hold arbitrary Ray Serve deployment settings), always
# present on a version (never null). Decode it with jsondecode() to reach
# specific keys instead of treating it as an opaque string, the same pattern
# used for anyscale_compute_config's advanced_instance_config/flags.
output "service_ray_serve_config" {
  value       = jsondecode(data.anyscale_service.by_id.primary_version.ray_serve_config)
  description = "The primary version's Ray Serve config, decoded from its JSON string"
}

output "service_status_checklist" {
  value       = data.anyscale_service.by_id.service_status_checklist
  description = "Per-component status breakdown (load balancer, cluster, etc.); null for terminated services or briefly after creation before the reconciler's first tick"
}

output "service_by_name_id" {
  value       = data.anyscale_service.by_name.id
  description = "The id resolved from the by-name lookup above"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `cloud_id` (String) The cloud ID this service belongs to. Can be used as a filter when looking up by name.
- `cloud_name` (String) The cloud name this service belongs to. Can be used as a filter when looking up by name. Will be resolved to `cloud_id`.
- `id` (String) The unique identifier of the service. Either `id` or `name` must be specified.
- `name` (String) The name of the service. Either `id` or `name` must be specified. This is an exact match: service names are unique only within a project (not organization-wide), so if the same name exists in more than one project, this lookup fails with an error asking you to set `project_id` (and/or `cloud_id`) to disambiguate, rather than silently guessing.
- `project_id` (String) The project ID this service belongs to. Can be used to narrow a lookup by `name` when the same name exists in more than one project.

### Read-Only

- `auto_rollout_enabled` (Boolean) Whether this service uses automatic rollout.
- `base_url` (String) The base URL of this service.
- `canary_version` (Attributes) The canary version of this service. Null unless the service is currently rolling out. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--canary_version))
- `created_at` (String) Timestamp when the service was created.
- `creator_id` (String) The ID of the user who created the service.
- `current_state` (String) The current state of this service (e.g. `RUNNING`, `UNHEALTHY`, `TERMINATED`). Ships as a plain string with no client-side enum validation, matching this provider's convention of not hand-maintaining a copy of the backend's enum list.
- `description` (String) Description of the service. Null if not set.
- `ended_at` (String) Timestamp when the service was terminated. Null while the service is active.
- `error_message` (String) Error message from processing the most recent API request against this service, if any. Null otherwise.
- `goal_state` (String) The goal state of this service (`RUNNING` or `TERMINATED`).
- `hostname` (String) The hostname of the service.
- `is_multi_version` (Boolean) Whether this service is a multi-version service (multiple active versions with no single canary).
- `primary_version` (Attributes) The primary version of this service. If the service is terminated, this refers to the most recently active version. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--primary_version))
- `service_observability_urls` (Attributes) Dashboard URLs for this service. Each URL is null if the backend has none to report (e.g. before the service's first successful deploy). (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--service_observability_urls))
- `service_status_checklist` (Attributes) Per-component status breakdown derived from the most recent reconciler snapshot. Null for terminated services and during the brief window before the reconciler's first tick on a brand-new service. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--service_status_checklist))

<a id="nestedatt--canary_version"></a>
### Nested Schema for `canary_version`

Read-Only:

- `build_id` (String) The ID of the cluster environment build (container image) this version runs.
- `compute_config_id` (String) The ID of the compute configuration this version uses.
- `connection_ids` (List of String) The connection IDs associated with this service version. Null if the API does not report connections for this version.
- `created_at` (String) Timestamp when this version was created.
- `current_state` (String) The current state of this service version (e.g. `RUNNING`, `STARTING`, `UNHEALTHY`).
- `current_weight` (Number) The current percentage of live traffic observed for this version, 0-100. Null if not currently observed.
- `id` (String) The unique identifier of this service version.
- `production_job_ids` (List of String) The production job IDs associated with this service version. Empty (not null) if none.
- `ray_serve_config` (String) The Ray Serve config for this version, as a JSON string. This is a dynamic, open-ended structure upstream with no fixed schema in this provider - use `jsondecode()` in HCL to access individual fields, the same convention `anyscale_compute_config`'s `advanced_instance_config`/`flags` use for similarly open-ended config. Always present (required upstream), even when its contents are trivial.
- `target_weight` (Number) The intended final traffic weight for this version, 0-100, when known. Null otherwise.
- `version` (String) The version string identifier for this version.
- `weight` (Number) The configured traffic weight currently stored for this version, 0-100. During rollouts this may be an intermediate desired load-balancer weight rather than the live figure - see `current_weight`.


<a id="nestedatt--primary_version"></a>
### Nested Schema for `primary_version`

Read-Only:

- `build_id` (String) The ID of the cluster environment build (container image) this version runs.
- `compute_config_id` (String) The ID of the compute configuration this version uses.
- `connection_ids` (List of String) The connection IDs associated with this service version. Null if the API does not report connections for this version.
- `created_at` (String) Timestamp when this version was created.
- `current_state` (String) The current state of this service version (e.g. `RUNNING`, `STARTING`, `UNHEALTHY`).
- `current_weight` (Number) The current percentage of live traffic observed for this version, 0-100. Null if not currently observed.
- `id` (String) The unique identifier of this service version.
- `production_job_ids` (List of String) The production job IDs associated with this service version. Empty (not null) if none.
- `ray_serve_config` (String) The Ray Serve config for this version, as a JSON string. This is a dynamic, open-ended structure upstream with no fixed schema in this provider - use `jsondecode()` in HCL to access individual fields, the same convention `anyscale_compute_config`'s `advanced_instance_config`/`flags` use for similarly open-ended config. Always present (required upstream), even when its contents are trivial.
- `target_weight` (Number) The intended final traffic weight for this version, 0-100, when known. Null otherwise.
- `version` (String) The version string identifier for this version.
- `weight` (Number) The configured traffic weight currently stored for this version, 0-100. During rollouts this may be an intermediate desired load-balancer weight rather than the live figure - see `current_weight`.


<a id="nestedatt--service_observability_urls"></a>
### Nested Schema for `service_observability_urls`

Read-Only:

- `serve_deployment_dashboard_embedding_url` (String) Embeddable variant of `serve_deployment_dashboard_url`.
- `serve_deployment_dashboard_url` (String) URL to a dashboard with graphs about a single deployment or replica of the service.
- `service_dashboard_embedding_url` (String) Embeddable variant of `service_dashboard_url`.
- `service_dashboard_url` (String) URL to a dashboard with graphs about the entire service.


<a id="nestedatt--service_status_checklist"></a>
### Nested Schema for `service_status_checklist`

Read-Only:

- `per_version` (Attributes List) Per-version components (cluster, application, target group), one entry per active service version. Empty (not null) if none. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--service_status_checklist--per_version))
- `shared` (Attributes List) Components shared across all versions (load balancer, listener rule, DNS, TLS certificate). Empty (not null) if none. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--service_status_checklist--shared))

<a id="nestedatt--service_status_checklist--per_version"></a>
### Nested Schema for `service_status_checklist.per_version`

Read-Only:

- `items` (Attributes List) Per-component statuses for this version. Empty (not null) if none. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--service_status_checklist--per_version--items))
- `version_id` (String) The service version these checklist items belong to.

<a id="nestedatt--service_status_checklist--per_version--items"></a>
### Nested Schema for `service_status_checklist.per_version.items`

Read-Only:

- `kind` (String) The kind of resource this checklist row represents (e.g. `SERVICE`, `SERVICE_VERSION`, `LOAD_BALANCER`). Ships as a plain string with no client-side enum validation, matching this provider's convention of not hand-maintaining a copy of the backend's enum list.
- `label` (String) User-facing label for this resource (e.g. `Cluster`, `Load Balancer`).
- `message` (String) Reconciler-provided message describing the current state. Empty string, not null, when the backend has no message to report.
- `observed_at` (String) When this row's state was last observed by the cluster manager. Null when the snapshot is missing a timestamp (older event).
- `state` (String) The state of this resource (e.g. `RUNNING`, `UNHEALTHY`, `STARTING`).
- `version_id` (String) The service version this item belongs to. Null for items shared across versions (e.g. cloud networking).



<a id="nestedatt--service_status_checklist--shared"></a>
### Nested Schema for `service_status_checklist.shared`

Read-Only:

- `kind` (String) The kind of resource this checklist row represents (e.g. `SERVICE`, `SERVICE_VERSION`, `LOAD_BALANCER`). Ships as a plain string with no client-side enum validation, matching this provider's convention of not hand-maintaining a copy of the backend's enum list.
- `label` (String) User-facing label for this resource (e.g. `Cluster`, `Load Balancer`).
- `message` (String) Reconciler-provided message describing the current state. Empty string, not null, when the backend has no message to report.
- `observed_at` (String) When this row's state was last observed by the cluster manager. Null when the snapshot is missing a timestamp (older event).
- `state` (String) The state of this resource (e.g. `RUNNING`, `UNHEALTHY`, `STARTING`).
- `version_id` (String) The service version this item belongs to. Null for items shared across versions (e.g. cloud networking).
